hero.desc: A hair transplant is not just hair. It is confidence, restored.
hero.subdesc: If your hairline keeps moving back, you are not alone. At Nexus Clinic Kuala Lumpur, we help you plan results that look natural.

trust.t1Desc: In the heart of Kuala Lumpur (Jalan Pinang, near KLCC)
trust.t2Desc: Two decades of excellence in aesthetic medicine

quickAnswers.title: Quick Answers People Want First
quickAnswers.desc: Everything you need to know before your consultation
quickAnswers.a1: In many cases, yes, because donor hair is usually more resistant to hair loss.
quickAnswers.a2: Most people feel pressure, not sharp pain, because local anaesthesia is used.
quickAnswers.a3: Full results often take 12 to 18 months.
quickAnswers.q4: How much does it cost in Malaysia?
quickAnswers.a4: From RM 5,500 to RM 30,000, depending on grafts and method.

methods.title: Hair Transplant Methods
methods.desc: FUE vs FUT vs DHI - Understanding your options
methods.fueDesc: Removes follicles one by one, then places them where needed.
methods.fueBenefit3: Works well for hairline and crown
methods.futDesc: Removes a thin strip from donor area, then separates grafts.
methods.dhiDesc: Placement style using an implanter tool for precise results.
methods.note: In real life, the "best" method depends on your hair loss pattern, donor strength, and goals.

whoFor.title: Who Is Hair Transplant For?
whoFor.wait1: Hair loss is still very fast
whoFor.waitNote: Start with non-surgical options first (PRP, medications, or laser), then re-check your progress.

areas.title: Areas We Can Treat
areas.desc: Not just the scalp - personalized restoration for every area

journey.title: Your Journey at Nexus Clinic
journey.s1Desc: We discuss your hair loss story, check donor area, density, and scalp health.
journey.s2Desc: A good hairline should fit your age and features. We design it together.
journey.s3Desc: Clear pricing by graft count or session. You know what you're paying for.
journey.s4Desc: Local anaesthesia, bring music or podcasts. We guide you through.
journey.s5Desc: Washing guidance, activity limits, and protected results.

recovery.title: Recovery Timeline
recovery.desc: What you will actually see, week by week
recovery.r1Desc: Scalp feels tight, swelling, gentle washing

prosCons.title: Honest View: Pros & Cons
prosCons.pro1: Uses your own hair, so it looks real
prosCons.pro2: Can improve hairline shape and coverage
prosCons.con2: May still lose native hair around transplant
prosCons.con4: Not everyone has enough donor supply
prosCons.note: A good clinic will say this clearly, before you commit.

cost.title: Hair Transplant Cost in Malaysia
cost.desc: Depending on grafts, method, and clinic's team

faq.q1: Is shedding after a hair transplant normal?
faq.a1: Yes, shedding can happen and it often worries people. It is commonly linked to shock loss, where hair shafts shed while follicles rest before growing again.
faq.a2: Most grafts survive when the plan is good and aftercare is followed. Risk factors include poor donor selection, smoking, uncontrolled health issues, and poor post-op care.
faq.q3: Can women get a hair transplant in Malaysia?
faq.a3: Yes, women can get hair transplants. The suitability depends on the pattern of hair loss and donor area strength.

cta.title: You Deserve an Honest Plan
cta.desc: A hair transplant can be life-changing, but only when it is planned well. Start with a proper assessment.
